log_entry_contract._comment: CANONICAL CROSS-LANGUAGE LOG ENTRY SCHEMA. This is the single source of truth for structured log events across all runtimes (Rust, TypeScript, Dart, Python). All emitters MUST conform to this schema before POSTing to POST /api/governor/log (RPC 740). sync_contracts.py generates typed LogEntry models in every language from this block.
log_entry_contract._storage_backend: SQLite LTM — activity_log table, /var/lib/horaizon/logs/activity.log (NDJSON rotation at 10MB)
log_entry_contract._ram_budget: ~1MB max — MPSC ring-buffer capped at 4096 entries × 256 bytes average

ts.description: Unix epoch milliseconds. Use monotonic wall clock (Date.now() in JS, DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch in Dart, SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH.elapsed() in Rust). Do NOT use a local/adjusted clock.

level._severity_order: TRACE < DEBUG < INFO < WARN < ERROR < CRITICAL
level.description: Log severity level. Must be one of the exact enum strings above — no lowercase, no abbreviations.

module.description: Source module identifier. Must exactly match the 'id' field in schemas/module_registry.json. Use 'client_flutter' for the Dart client.

subsystem.description: Sub-component within the module. Use snake_case. Examples: 'proxy_handler', 'cgroups', 'sdui_compiler', 'diary_repository', 'governor_client'.

msg.description: Human-readable description of the event. Should be terse and actionable. Do NOT include sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII).
msg.example: Loaded blueprint for screen diary_list in 1.2ms

telemetry.description: Arbitrary key-value performance metadata. All values must be JSON-serializable primitives. No nested objects.

known_keys.latency_ms: f64 — request/operation round-trip latency in milliseconds
known_keys.latency_us: u64 — request latency in microseconds (preferred for sub-ms precision)
known_keys.memory_kb: u64 — current process RSS memory in kilobytes
known_keys.cpu_temp_c: f32 — Pi 5 SoC temperature in Celsius
known_keys.payload_bytes: u64 — raw byte size of request/response body

trace_id.description: 8-character lowercase hex correlation ID. Shared across a single logical request that spans multiple modules (e.g. a Flutter action that triggers Governor → shua_diary → SQLite). Generate with first 8 chars of a UUID v4.

validation_rules._comment: Governor MUST reject log entries violating these rules with HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity.
validation_rules.rule_1: 'level' must be one of the exact enum strings. Reject with code 'INVALID_LEVEL' if not.
validation_rules.rule_2: 'module' must match a known module ID. Reject with code 'UNKNOWN_MODULE' if not.
validation_rules.rule_3: 'ts' must be within ±60 seconds of the Governor's current system clock. Reject with code 'TIMESTAMP_SKEW' if outside this window (prevents log replay attacks).
validation_rules.rule_4: 'msg' must not exceed 512 bytes UTF-8. Truncate silently server-side if over limit (never reject — log truncation is better than log loss).
validation_rules.rule_5: 'telemetry' values must be primitive types only (string, number, boolean, null). Reject nested objects with code 'INVALID_TELEMETRY'.
validation_rules.rule_6: If 'trace_id' is present, it must match the pattern '^[0-9a-f]{8}$'. Reject with code 'INVALID_TRACE_ID' if malformed.

emitter_rules._comment: Rules governing emitter behaviour in each language. These are NEVER relaxed.
emitter_rules.rule_fire_and_forget: All non-Rust emitters (TypeScript, Dart, Python) MUST use fire-and-forget HTTP POST. They must NEVER await the response on a hot path. Network errors must be silently discarded — a logging failure must NEVER crash or stall the calling module.
emitter_rules.rule_no_sensitive_data: Passwords, auth tokens, device UUIDs, and PII must NEVER appear in 'msg' or 'telemetry'. Use '[REDACTED]' if context requires referencing a sensitive field.
emitter_rules.rule_governor_unreachable: If the Governor is unreachable, emitters must fail silently. They may buffer up to 32 entries in memory and retry after 10 seconds. Buffers must be discarded, not persisted, on process restart.
emitter_rules.rule_rust_direct: The Rust Governor logs its own events directly into the MPSC ring-buffer via the 'tracing' subscriber. It must NOT POST to its own HTTP endpoint (avoids self-loop).
emitter_rules.rule_no_spam: Emitters must apply basic deduplication: if the same (level, module, subsystem, msg) tuple is emitted more than 10 times within 1 second, collapse into a single entry with telemetry 'occurrence_count' set to the actual count.

sqlite_schema._comment: Governor creates this table on first boot via rusqlite migration. Indexes enable O(log n) lookups for Flutter Diagnostics Panel queries.
indexes: CREATE INDEX idx_activity_ts     ON activity_log(ts DESC)

http_query_params._comment: Query parameters for GET /api/logs (RPC 741). All params are optional.
http_query_params.module: string — filter by source module ID
http_query_params.level: string — minimum severity level filter (inclusive, following severity order)
http_query_params.trace_id: string — filter by correlation trace ID
http_query_params.since_ts: u64 — Unix ms lower bound timestamp
http_query_params.until_ts: u64 — Unix ms upper bound timestamp
http_query_params.limit: u16 — max entries to return, default 50, max 500

log_levels._comment: Predefined HBP logging levels as compact integers.

log_tags._comment: 24-bit predefined system log tag bitmask flags. sync_contracts.py generates these constants in all targets.
